name: Crab Cakes with Basil Mayonnaise
recipeInstructions:
1. Blanch basil leaves in a saucepan of boiling water for 30 seconds.
2. Drain the leaves and transfer them to an ice water bath to cool.
3. Pat the basil dry on paper towels.
4. Finely chop the basil.
5. Mix mayonnaise, mustard, lemon juice, and cayenne together. Set aside 1/2 cup of mayo for the crab cakes. Mix the chopped basil into the remainder and refrigerate.
6. Heat olive oil in a large heavy skillet over medium heat.
7. Add celery and onion to the skillet and sauté until tender (about 5 minutes).
8. Transfer the vegetables to a bowl.
9. Stir in the crabmeat, breadcrumbs, chives, and reserved mayonnaise. Season with salt and pepper.
10. Using about 1/3 cup of mixture for each, form into 12 cakes that are approximately 2 1/2 inches in diameter.
11. Arrange the cakes on a baking sheet.
12. Place flour in a small bowl.
13. Whisk eggs in another bowl.
14. Place remaining breadcrumbs in a third bowl.
15. Bread each cake in the following order: flour, eggs, then breadcrumbs.
16. Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
17. Add crab cakes in batches, cooking until golden. Add more oil as required.
18. Serve with basil mayonnaise.
